title: Cache Hit/Miss Simulation
description: Pseudocode to simulate cache access and calculate hit rate
code:
// Cache Hit/Miss Simulation
CACHE_SIZE = 1024 // lines
BLOCK_SIZE = 64 // bytes
associativity = 1 // direct-mapped

cache = array[CACHE_SIZE] of {valid, tag, data}
accesses = 0
hits = 0
misses = 0

function access_memory(address):
 accesses = accesses + 1
 index = (address / BLOCK_SIZE) % CACHE_SIZE
 tag = address / (CACHE_SIZE * BLOCK_SIZE)
 
 if cache[index].valid AND cache[index].tag == tag:
 hits = hits + 1
 return cache[index].data // HIT
 else:
 misses = misses + 1
 load_block_from_memory(address, cache[index])
 return cache[index].data // MISS

function calculate_efficiency():
 hit_rate = (hits / accesses) * 100
 miss_rate = (misses / accesses) * 100
 print("Hit Rate:", hit_rate, "%")
 print("Miss Rate:", miss_rate, "%")
explanation: This code simulates a direct-mapped cache, tracking hits and misses for sequential or random memory accesses to measure cache efficiency

title: LRU Replacement Policy Implementation
description: Implementation of Least Recently Used replacement for set-associative cache
explanation: This implements LRU replacement for a set-associative cache by tracking access times with a global counter and finding the least recently used block on misses
